title: Argentinian Lamb Empanadas
description: A classic Argentinian dish, these lamb empanadas are perfect for a dinner party. The flaky pastry and flavorful lamb filling will leave your guests wanting more.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat a couple of tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until it is no longer pink.
Add the cumin and paprika and cook for 1 minute.
Roll out the empanada dough and cut out circles of dough.
Place a tablespoon of the lamb mixture onto one half of each dough circle.
Fold the other half of the dough over the filling and press the edges together with a fork.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the egg wash.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
